title: BALANCING SPIRITUAL & MATERIAL DUTIES | HG AMITASANA DAS | GITA LIFE Live

The topic what we have selected for discussion today is a very interesting topic, very relevant for all the people who are trying to practice spiritual life while engaged in their so-called worldly duties.
We often find difficult to balance the two.
Because.
Our material obligations and duties also expect quality time from us to do justice to those duties.
Other side, we also understand that our spiritual pursuits also need a lot of energy, quality time.
And at times we might find ourselves riding on two boats.
With one leg on a boat heading towards material direction.
And one leg on a boat heading towards an opposite direction.
And it may not be a very, very pleasant experience.
So today the discussion is all about that, how we can strike a healthy balance.

So, when we get up in the morning, as I said, few important things which we have to take care
of our spirit. One thing for sure, we have to constantly work on improving the quality of our
chanting.
Because in Kali Yuga in our present times, the recommended process for our spiritual growth
is Harinama, chanting of the Lord's holy names. So, constantly we should strive. Prabhupada has
given us the magic figure of 16 rounds. I am sure many of us, some of us may be chanting 16 rounds,
some of us maybe just started one round, two rounds, five rounds, eight rounds.
Constantly we should endeavor
to improve the quantity,
quantity and the quality of our rounds. No doubt going beyond 16 may be a challenge for a person
who is having lot of his worldly duties but there are many people, many initiated devotees who are
working, who are having their jobs, who are initiated, who have taken diksha, who are
chanting 16 rounds and also trying to do their worldly duties. So, we should aspire to come to
that magic number, chant 16 rounds and constantly, first quantity and then constantly endeavour to
improve the quality of our rounds and the quality of rounds, perfection, there is no limit. We can

Shravanam could be in the form of reading Srila Prabhupada s books,

maybe we might feel very good after hearing to them, but purity is the force.

that can happen if we hear from pure devotees. So, these are few activities,
chanting, hearing, association, reading Srila Prabhupada books, offering some

for us to balance our material and spiritual duties, our life can be like

and even when we come back from our work before we retire before we sleep
generally Prabhupada recommended in the temples that there should be kirtans in

like my spiritually also we may get so to say our charge spiritual charge might

worldly duties. If we have engaged in our morning sadhana nicely, spent quality time,
quality chanting, quality reading, good association, then there's a good possibility that
we will remember the Lord, we will engage in right kind of activities,
we will be able to, we will have the strength to make the right choices throughout the day.
You know life throws up lot of choices, every moment there's a choice to do this or that,

So we have the upper layer, we have the bottom layer and if we have spent quality time with

Krishna Consciousness will permeate throughout the day.
When opportunities come, we will take that opportunities to also incorporate some spiritual activities.
For example, when I was working, we used to have lunch break for about 45 minutes.
So, I would finish my lunch in about 15 minutes, quickly.
And then half an hour, every day during lunch time, I would read Shri Prabhupada Leela Amrita.
I finished some five or six volumes just in the office.
Every day during lunch time, my friends would be going out.
They would go out for lunch and they would be talking, discussing news, talking about cricket, all that.
So, I would take the packed lunch from home. I was not eating outside.
And I would quickly finish the lunch and read 30 minutes.
So, like that, how do we balance?
While we engage in worldly duties, if we are properly placed with the right consciousness,
there are a lot of pockets of time.
For example, we are travelling to the office. We are stuck in a traffic jam.
Or for that matter, we are taking an elevator.
We have three minutes, five minutes. We can chant the names of the Lord.
So, that way, we can strike a good balance.
Start the day with spiritual activities.
End the day with spiritual activities.
And throughout the day, as far as possible, we try to engage in some kind of remembrance of the Lord.
Some kind of activities which will help us be more conscious of the Lord.
And if we do that, gradually, as I said, Jnana and Vairagya, the real knowledge will awaken.
Our consciousness will get purified and Vairagya detachment will also come about.
So, this is how we should strive to balance.
And always pray to Prabhupāda, pray to Kṛṣṇa to give us the right direction, right association.

Yes, we should get up early in the morning, but we require their blessings.
We require their mercy.

So, we can offer prayers to Prabhupāda.
We can offer prayers to the Lord.
To give their mercy, to beg their mercy, so that we can strike a good, healthy balance.
To give justice to this human form of life.

The child is about to return home.
The child is about to return.
Let me cook this.
Let me prepare his bedroom.

That will help us grow.
Grow spiritually.
So it is not the nature of job per se.
Even if a person is a software engineer, a person is working in an industry, it has nothing to do with Krishna, still we can try to spiritualize our existence.
